lever_summary: Dense Bool tensor storage. Added a LiteralBufferStorage::Bool { values: Arc<Vec<bool>>, lazy literals } variant mirroring F64/I64, so bool tensors carry 1 byte/element instead of the 24-byte Literal enum. TensorValue::new_bool_values + Value::vector_bool now build dense bool; the bool full-reduction (eval_reduce_bitwise_axes, DType::Bool reduce_all) folds as_bool_slice() directly instead of matching Literal::Bool per element.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / succinct typed storage (1-byte bool vs 24-byte tagged union)
alien_graveyard.mapping: store boolean masks contiguously so boolean reductions/logic fold a byte slice the compiler can vectorize, instead of materializing + matching a tagged-union Vec<Literal>

method: Same-worker comparison on vmi1167313: dense (vector_bool) input vs a Vec<Literal>-backed reference run in the same process.
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- reduce_and_64k_bool --save-baseline boolred

change.lever: fj-core: LiteralBufferStorage::Bool variant + from_bool_values / as_bool_slice / as_slice lazy-materialize / len / make_mut COW / Clone / owned IntoIterator arms; TensorValue::new_bool_values; Value::vector_bool routes through it. fj-lax: the DType::Bool reduce_all branch in eval_reduce_bitwise_axes gains an as_bool_slice() fast fold, falling back to the unchanged Literal loop.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: contiguous bool slice folded in ascending order, identical to the Vec<Literal> loop
isomorphism.bool_op: unchanged: same bool_init seed and bool_op (ReduceAnd &&, ReduceOr ||) applied in the same order
isomorphism.storage_observability: as_slice() lazily materializes the identical Vec<Literal::Bool> on demand, so Debug/PartialEq/serde/Deref/indexing and every existing consumer see byte-identical Literals; mutation COW-materializes to Literals like F64/I64
isomorphism.errors: as_bool_slice() is Some only for Bool dense storage (element count matches shape); Literal-backed/malformed Bool tensors return None and run the unchanged Literal loop (which still type-checks each element); output is scalar_bool

validation.name: fj_core_lib_suite
validation.result: passed: 130 tests (vector_bool now dense, no regressions)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-core -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
validation.result: passed (run with --release to dodge a shared-target proc-macro rmeta drift in debug)
